boundaries.title_guess: Cornwall: Fowey (END only — trip continues from previous window)

evidence.excerpt: crossed the R. Fowey at Fowey on the car ferry ... The campsite ... Basic + clean
we gave up + set off for home. We telephoned Peter ahead ... arrival at 7.35

boundaries.mixed_detail: Window opens mid-trip; this is the tail end of the 2003 Eden Project / Cornwall / Fowey trip (book4 index p79-81). Trip ends on A_0337_R with the drive home.

boundaries.title_guess: Manchester & Tatton Park (Quarry Bank Mill, Styal, Knutsford)

evidence.excerpt: Saturday May 3rd. We left at 3 pm, Simon, Eve, Tonic, to go north. Quarry Bank Mill & the Styal Estate are just south of Manchester
we drove Thomas & Owen back to Manchester & set off home, having tea at Keele services

boundaries.title_guess: Malvern (school concert/speech day) & Llanafan Fawr, Wales

evidence.excerpt: Friday May 23rd. We left at 5.35. (Simon, Eve & Tonic). The motorway traffic was stationary so we went on back roads to Malvern for the 7. concert
the 3 of us have come to Llanafan Fawr. There is a church & pub here & lots of sheep ... south of Elan Valley near Builth Wells

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0338_R is mixed: top continues/ends the Manchester trip ('set off home, having tea at Keele services'); the break falls at 'Friday May 23rd. We left at 5.35.' which starts the Malvern concert + Llanafan Fawr trip.

boundaries.title_guess: Malvern day visits (Jamie's farm; Reg Farrar's drinks party)

evidence.excerpt: Tuesday. We left at 9.15 ... We reached Jamie's farm ... After leaving the farm we went to Holloway's garden shop ... We drove on home.
June 15th Sunday. After the 10.30. we drove down to Malvern, to Reg Farrar's, for a drinks party.

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0340_R is mixed: the 'Tuesday' paragraph ends the Llanafan Fawr/Malvern trip with the drive home; the break falls at 'June 15th Sunday' which is a short same-day drive to Malvern (Reg Farrar's drinks party) — appears non-overnight / not a van outing. Low-ish confidence on its trip status. NOTE: spread A_0341 is a near-duplicate re-transcription of A_0340 (same page no 85) — same content, not a separate boundary.

boundaries.title_guess: Cambridge (King's College May Ball, William & Bryony)

evidence.excerpt: Tuesday June 17th. Simon + Eve set off at 4 for Cambridge + parked at William's house, 7 Maddingley Rd ... May Ball
After going to bed at 3 am, we left at 7.45. for the factory and home.

boundaries.title_guess: Germany: Bad Honnef, Alsfeld, Eisenach (Wartburg), Erfurt, Weimar

evidence.excerpt: Friday 1st August. Simon, Eve, Thomas, John Charles left at 6 for Germany ... On to Ashford in Kent, to Broad Hembury Campsite (OK for using en route to Tunnel). 10pm
We drove through France, Belgium, Holland + into Germany ... arriving at Bad Honnef

boundaries.mixed_detail: A_0342_R is mixed: top ends the Cambridge trip ('we left at 7.45. for the factory and home'); the break falls at 'Friday 1st August. Simon, Eve, Thomas, John Charles left at 6 for Germany' which starts the major 2003 Germany trip (Ashford -> Tunnel -> Bad Honnef -> Alsfeld -> Eisenach/Wartburg -> Erfurt -> Weimar). This trip runs A_0343-A_0349 and does not show a return home within this window.

boundaries.title_guess: Normandy: Pegasus Bridge & Caen (out-of-sequence / misfiled 2001 page)

evidence.excerpt: Pegasus Bridge from the air ... 00.19 hrs June 6th 1944
We drove on to Caen ... the Abbaye aux Dames ... Abbaye aux Hommes ... camped in some trees beside the racecourse

boundaries.mixed_detail: Date/narrative discontinuity: A_0349 is still in Germany (2003, Weimar); A_0350 jumps to a Normandy trip. Page no on A_0350_R is 45, matching book4 index p44-45 (Deauville/Pegasus Bridge, Caen, 2001 marker). This page appears physically out of sequence — a 2001 Normandy trip fragment bound in among the 2003 Germany pages — rather than a continuation of the Germany trip.

open_at_end: Germany 2003 trip starting A_0342_R runs through A_0349 and shows no return home before the window ends; A_0350 is a separate out-of-sequence Normandy (2001) page rather than its continuation.
notes: Page numbering in the Germany section is inconsistent (A_0343=87, A_0344=88, A_0348=72) suggesting re-pagination, but narrative continuity across A_0343-A_0349 is clear. Spreads A_0340 and A_0341 are near-duplicate transcriptions of the same physical spread (both page no 85); the A_0340_R mixed boundary covers both.
